title: glechoma depauperata
intro: Glechoma depauperata, also known as the Alpine gypsywort or small gypsywort, is a perennial flowering plant species in the mint family, Lamiaceae. It is native to the mountainous regions of Europe, where it grows in rocky areas, particularly in the high Alps. The plant is used in traditional medicine, where its leaves and stems are valued for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the leaves of Glechoma depauperata are often used to make teas and infusions that are said to have health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and promoting respiratory health.
